participants.name: Jaeha Lee
participants.bio: Building AI-powered tools for human connection. Background in full-stack engineering with Python and React. Interested in knowledge graphs, LLM agents, and making conferences more meaningful. Currently working on Who-to-Meet at Ralphthon hackathon. Believes in transparent AI that explains its reasoning.

participants.name: Soyeon Kim
participants.bio: Product designer turned AI researcher. Spent 5 years at Samsung working on UX for mobile apps, now exploring how LLMs can generate personalized user interfaces. Passionate about accessible design and Korean typography. Runs a small design community in Seoul.
goals: launch an AI design tool startup

participants.name: Marcus Chen
participants.bio: Serial entrepreneur, 3rd startup. Previously built a fintech analytics platform (acquired). Now exploring AI agents for enterprise workflow automation. Strong believer in small teams shipping fast. Stanford CS, lived in SF for 10 years, recently moved to Seoul for the tech scene.

participants.name: Yuna Park
participants.bio: ML engineer at Naver, specializing in NLP for Korean language. Built the entity extraction pipeline for Naver's search. Interested in multilingual LLMs and how they handle Korean-English code-switching. Weekend project: building a Korean podcast transcription tool.

participants.name: David Okonkwo
participants.bio: DevRel at a YC startup building open-source observability tools. Previously a backend engineer at Stripe. Loves building developer communities and creating technical content. Runs a popular YouTube channel on distributed systems. Interested in how AI changes developer workflows.

participants.name: Minji Cho
participants.bio: Data scientist working on recommendation systems at Kakao. Background in graph neural networks and collaborative filtering. Fascinated by how social networks form at events. Side project: building a real-time networking app that suggests who to talk to at conferences based on mutual interests.

participants.name: Alex Rivera
participants.bio: Indie game developer and creative coder. Makes generative art with code and builds experimental games that explore social dynamics. Published 3 indie games on Steam. Currently prototyping a game where AI NPCs form their own social networks. Uses Unity and p5.js.

participants.name: Jihoon Kang
participants.bio: Blockchain engineer exploring the intersection of decentralized identity and AI. Previously at Klaytn foundation. Now building a protocol for verifiable AI agent interactions. Believes AI agents need provable identity and reputation systems. Avid rock climber.

participants.name: Sarah Mitchell
participants.bio: Cognitive scientist turned AI ethicist. Researching how people form trust with AI systems at KAIST. Published papers on AI transparency and explainable recommendations. Believes every AI decision shown to users must be accompanied by clear reasoning. Hosts a podcast on human-AI trust.
goals: collaborate with AI builders on ethics

participants.name: Taemin Lee
participants.bio: Full-stack developer and hackathon enthusiast. Won 5 hackathons in the past year. Specializes in rapid prototyping with Next.js and Supabase. Currently interested in building tools that help people connect meaningfully at tech events. Also teaches coding workshops for beginners.

participants.name: Hana Yoshida
participants.bio: UX researcher specializing in conversational AI interfaces. Worked at LINE on chatbot design. Now freelancing and researching how chat-based interfaces can feel more natural and less transactional. Fluent in Japanese, Korean, and English. Passionate about cross-cultural communication patterns.
goals: design the ideal AI chat interface

participants.name: Ryan Park
participants.bio: Cloud architect at AWS Korea. Helps startups design scalable infrastructure. Deep knowledge of serverless patterns and event-driven architecture. Interested in how AI workloads change infrastructure needs. Runs the Seoul Cloud Native meetup. Weekend barista.
goals: open a specialty coffee shop someday

participants.name: Eunji Bae
participants.bio: Computational linguist building Korean language tools. Created an open-source Korean morphological analyzer that's used by 500+ developers. Interested in how LLMs handle agglutinative languages differently. Currently researching prompt engineering techniques specific to Korean.

participants.name: Chris Nakamura
participants.bio: Music technologist and creative AI researcher. Building tools that help musicians collaborate with AI for composition and production. Background in audio engineering and machine learning. Released an AI-assisted album. Interested in the intersection of creativity and technology.

participants.name: Jisoo Han
participants.bio: Venture partner at a Seoul-based fund focused on AI and deep tech. Previously founded and exited a B2B SaaS company. Evaluates 200+ startups per year. Interested in AI agents, developer tools, and Korean language AI. Believes the next wave of great companies will be built in Asia.
goals: find next great AI startup to back

participants.name: Luna Kim
participants.bio: Neuroscience PhD student at SNU researching attention mechanisms in the brain and how they relate to transformer architectures. Fascinated by biological vs artificial intelligence. Builds brain-computer interface prototypes as a hobby. Also a competitive StarCraft player.

participants.name: Daniel Kwon
participants.bio: Product manager at Toss (Korean fintech unicorn). Shipped payment features used by millions. Now interested in how AI can personalize financial products. Strong believer in user-centric design and data-driven decisions. Runs marathons and tracks everything with data.

participants.name: Mina Jeong
participants.bio: Open-source maintainer and Rust evangelist. Core contributor to a popular async runtime. Passionate about systems programming and performance optimization. Currently building a high-performance vector database in Rust. Runs Rust Seoul meetup. Cat person.
goals: find users and contributors for OSS project

participants.name: Tommy Zhao
participants.bio: AI art director and former graphic designer. Creates AI-generated visual campaigns for major brands. Pioneering the use of diffusion models in commercial design workflows. Teaching a course on AI art direction at a Seoul design school. Interested in the future of creative professions.
goals: define AI art direction as a discipline
find tech co-founder for design tool

participants.name: Hyejin Ryu
participants.bio: Healthcare AI researcher building diagnostic tools with medical imaging. Works at a university hospital developing AI that detects early-stage cancers. Passionate about making AI in healthcare trustworthy and explainable. Volunteers to teach AI literacy at community centers.
goals: improve AI explainability in clinical settings
